checks.finding: REQ-006 through REQ-015 identify inspectable artifacts, preservation behavior, compatibility behavior, profile exclusions, parity, and release metadata; AC-001 through AC-014 plus the behavioral and layer contracts provide observable validation targets.
Every goal REQ-006 through REQ-015 is referenced by at least one matching acceptance criterion in the acceptance-test matrix.
AC-001 through AC-014 specify observable file content, diagnostics, exit status, preservation behavior, test outcomes, parity, and metadata consistency.

The Non-goals section explicitly excludes direct Figma integration, automated visualization or image analysis, new review loops and identifiers, sdd-ship and quality-gate changes, LITE model changes, Mermaid rendering, and a dedicated backend layer file.

The requirements state backward compatibility, non-overwrite behavior, LITE exclusion, Bash/PowerShell equivalence, version consistency, traversal rejection, local-only visual inputs, and preservation of review-gate ordering.

The Risks section states that review-loop manifests may reject unlisted layer artifacts and that tests must prove the intended allowed-input behavior, but AC-001 through AC-014 and the behavioral contracts do not define which review-loop manifests must accept which new canonical artifacts or the expected acceptance/rejection outcome. A task author or verifier therefore cannot determine the required compatibility fixture or pass condition. This belongs in specification review because the missing validation outcome must be defined before design and task decomposition, and it is Major because an unresolved manifest mismatch could prevent the existing review gates from consuming the generated specification set.
